internal_anchors.details: All 75 internal detail links (#winNNN) resolve. All showTab() calls target valid tab IDs. Test suite (2028 tests) covers internal link resolution comprehensively.

tab_structure.details: 9 tabs: overview → evaluate → model → wins → pages → predictions → falsify → selftest → ai → refs. Chain is continuous with no breaks. All tabs have section-nav with correct prev/next links. Heading hierarchy valid (no skipped levels).

external_links.details: 45 non-DOI external links checked. 43 returned OK. 2 ScienceDirect URLs returned 403 (bot-blocking, not actual broken links — these are publisher domain academic article pages that block automated requests). All data source links (NOAA, NASA, ESA, USGS, INTERMAGNET, etc.) are reachable.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json has 67 entries, HTML references all 67. Verdict counts (Refuted:9, Self-Contradicted:13, Misleading:21, Std Model Explains:16, Not Demonstrated:4, Unfalsifiable:4) are computed at build time from wins.json via {{PLACEHOLDER}} tokens in sections.json. code_analysis: 67 reviewed, 0 pending. Build reproducibility confirms counts match.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 67 WINs have detail anchors (id='winNNN' format). All WIN-NNN references appear in the summary table. All detail_group values are consistent across grouped WINs.

build_reproducibility.details: Rebuilt HTML via 'node build.js html' — output is byte-identical to current docs/index.html. No build drift.

summary: All checks pass. The site is structurally sound: 2028 automated tests pass, the build is reproducible with zero diff, all 9 tabs are correctly chained, all 67 WIN detail anchors resolve, all data-prose counts are consistent, and all 45 non-DOI external links are reachable. Two ScienceDirect URLs return 403 due to bot-blocking but are not actually broken — these are publisher-domain links that arguably fall under the DOI/publisher skip list.
